description: Mathematical finance theorems: extended Black-Scholes (put, digitals, vega, rho, forward), Bachelier model, implied volatility, Black-76 formula for futures, single-period binomial replication. All derived from standard Gaussian / discrete-time hypotheses; no Itô calculus required.

theorems.name: Black-Scholes European Put Formula
theorems.description: Discounted expected put payoff under the risk-neutral lognormal hypothesis: P = K e^{-rT} Phi(-d_2) - S_0 Phi(-d_1), where d_1, d_2 are the standard BS quantities.

code.lean:
import Mathlib
import HybridVerify.BlackScholesPut

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real
open scoped NNReal
open HybridVerify

variable {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω}

/-- Black-Scholes European put pricing formula via direct integration
    parallel to the call formula (left-tail completing-the-square). -/
theorem bs_put_formula
    {Q : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure Q]
    {S_0 K r σ T : ℝ} {Z : Ω → ℝ}
    (h : BSCallHyp Q S_0 K r σ T Z) :
    ∫ ω, Real.exp (-r * T) * max (K - bsTerminal S_0 r σ T (Z ω)) 0 ∂Q
      = K * Real.exp (-r * T) * Phi (-(bsd2 S_0 K r σ T)) -
        S_0 * Phi (-(bsd1 S_0 K r σ T)) :=
  HybridVerify.bs_put_formula h

metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Lives in lean/HybridVerify/BlackScholesPut.lean (~230 lines). Derivation parallel to bs_call_formula: HasLaw transfer to standard normal, max(K - S_T, 0) as indicator on Iic(-d_2), then left-tail completing-the-square integral_exp_mul_gaussianPDFReal_Iic. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: Put-Call Parity
theorems.description: C - P = S_0 - K e^{-rT}: the difference between European call and put prices under the BS hypothesis equals the spot minus the discounted strike. Direct algebraic corollary of bs_call_formula + bs_put_formula + Phi(d) + Phi(-d) = 1.

code.lean:
import Mathlib
import HybridVerify.BlackScholesPut

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real
open scoped NNReal
open HybridVerify

variable {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω}

/-- Put-call parity: C - P = S_0 - K e^{-rT}. -/
theorem put_call_parity
    {Q : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure Q]
    {S_0 K r σ T : ℝ} {Z : Ω → ℝ}
    (h : BSCallHyp Q S_0 K r σ T Z) :
    (∫ ω, Real.exp (-r * T) * max (bsTerminal S_0 r σ T (Z ω) - K) 0 ∂Q) -
    (∫ ω, Real.exp (-r * T) * max (K - bsTerminal S_0 r σ T (Z ω)) 0 ∂Q)
      = S_0 - K * Real.exp (-r * T) :=
  HybridVerify.bs_put_call_parity h

metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Direct algebraic corollary of bs_call_formula + bs_put_formula + Phi_add_Phi_neg. Lives in lean/HybridVerify/BlackScholesPut.lean. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: Cash-or-Nothing Digital Call
theorems.description: Cash-or-nothing digital pays $1 iff S_T > K. Price: V = e^{-rT} Phi(d_2).

code.lean:
import Mathlib
import HybridVerify.BlackScholesDigital

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real
open scoped NNReal
open HybridVerify

variable {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω}

/-- Cash-or-nothing digital call pricing formula. -/
theorem cash_or_nothing_formula
    {Q : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure Q]
    {S_0 K r σ T : ℝ} {Z : Ω → ℝ}
    (h : BSCallHyp Q S_0 K r σ T Z) :
    ∫ ω, Real.exp (-r * T) *
        (Set.Ioi K).indicator (fun _ => (1 : ℝ)) (bsTerminal S_0 r σ T (Z ω)) ∂Q
      = Real.exp (-r * T) * Phi (bsd2 S_0 K r σ T) :=
  HybridVerify.bs_cash_or_nothing_formula h

metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. HasLaw transfer + indicator identification on Ioi(-d_2) via bsTerminal_gt_K_iff + gaussianReal_Ioi_toReal. Lives in lean/HybridVerify/BlackScholesDigital.lean. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: Asset-or-Nothing Digital Call
theorems.description: Asset-or-nothing digital pays S_T iff S_T > K. Price: V = S_0 Phi(d_1).

code.lean:
import Mathlib
import HybridVerify.BlackScholesDigital

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real
open scoped NNReal
open HybridVerify

variable {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω}

/-- Asset-or-nothing digital call pricing formula. -/
theorem asset_or_nothing_formula
    {Q : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure Q]
    {S_0 K r σ T : ℝ} {Z : Ω → ℝ}
    (h : BSCallHyp Q S_0 K r σ T Z) :
    ∫ ω, Real.exp (-r * T) *
        (Set.Ioi K).indicator (fun s => s) (bsTerminal S_0 r σ T (Z ω)) ∂Q
      = S_0 * Phi (bsd1 S_0 K r σ T) :=
  HybridVerify.bs_asset_or_nothing_formula h

metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. HasLaw transfer + indicator on Ioi(-d_2) + completing-the-square integral_exp_mul_gaussianPDFReal_Ioi. Together with cash-or-nothing, gives BS call decomposition. Lives in lean/HybridVerify/BlackScholesDigital.lean. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: Forward / Futures Pricing Formula
theorems.description: F = S_0 e^{rT}: the no-arbitrage forward price equals the risk-neutral expectation of the terminal asset price under BSCallHyp.

code.lean:
import Mathlib
import HybridVerify.BlackScholesForward

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real
open scoped NNReal
open HybridVerify

variable {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω}

/-- No-arbitrage forward price under BS lognormal hypothesis: E_Q[S_T] = S_0 e^{rT}. -/
theorem forward_price
    {Q : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure Q]
    {S_0 K r σ T : ℝ} {Z : Ω → ℝ}
    (h : BSCallHyp Q S_0 K r σ T Z) :
    ∫ ω, bsTerminal S_0 r σ T (Z ω) ∂Q = S_0 * Real.exp (r * T) :=
  HybridVerify.expected_terminal_eq_forward h

metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Uses Gaussian MGF identity integral_exp_mul_gaussianPDFReal_univ + BS martingale algebra. Lives in lean/HybridVerify/BlackScholesForward.lean. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: BS Vega (Sensitivity to Volatility)
theorems.description: Vega = dV/dσ = S phi(d_1) sqrt(T). The Black-Scholes call price has derivative with respect to volatility equal to S * pdf(d_1) * sqrt(T), strictly positive for S, T > 0.

code.lean:
import Mathlib
import HybridVerify.BlackScholesPDE

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real
open scoped NNReal
open HybridVerify

/-- Black-Scholes vega: dV/dσ = S phi(d_1) sqrt(τ). -/
theorem bs_vega {K r : ℝ} (hK : 0 < K)
    {S σ τ : ℝ} (hS : 0 < S) (hσ : 0 < σ) (hτ : 0 < τ) :
    HasDerivAt (fun s => HybridVerify.bsV K r s S τ)
      (S * gaussianPDFReal 0 1 (HybridVerify.bsd1 S K r σ τ) * Real.sqrt τ) σ :=
  HybridVerify.hasDerivAt_bsV_sigma hK hS hσ hτ

metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Chain rule + magic identity bs_identity collapse the d/dσ d_i terms, leaving S pdf(d_1) sqrt(τ). Lives in lean/HybridVerify/BlackScholesPDE.lean. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: BS Rho (Sensitivity to Risk-Free Rate)
theorems.description: Rho = dV/dr = K tau e^{-r tau} Phi(d_2). The Black-Scholes call price derivative with respect to the risk-free rate r equals K * tau * exp(-r*tau) * Phi(d_2).

code.lean:
import Mathlib
import HybridVerify.BlackScholesPDE

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real
open scoped NNReal
open HybridVerify

/-- Black-Scholes rho: dV/dr = K tau e^{-r tau} Phi(d_2). -/
theorem bs_rho {K σ τ : ℝ} (hK : 0 < K) (hσ : 0 < σ) (hτ : 0 < τ)
    {S : ℝ} (hS : 0 < S) (r : ℝ) :
    HasDerivAt (fun r' => HybridVerify.bsV K r' σ S τ)
      (K * τ * Real.exp (-(r * τ)) * HybridVerify.Phi (HybridVerify.bsd2 S K r σ τ)) r :=
  HybridVerify.hasDerivAt_bsV_r hK hσ hτ hS r

metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Chain rule on d/dr d_i + d/dr exp(-rτ) + magic identity collapses pdf terms, leaving K*τ*exp(-rτ)*Phi(d_2). Lives in lean/HybridVerify/BlackScholesPDE.lean. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: Bachelier Model Call Pricing
theorems.description: Under arithmetic-BM dynamics S_T = S_0 + sigma*sqrt(T)*Z (no log, no exponential), the European call price is V = (S_0 - K) Phi(d) + sigma sqrt(T) phi(d), where d = (S_0 - K)/(sigma sqrt(T)).

code.lean:
import Mathlib
import HybridVerify.BachelierModel

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real
open scoped NNReal
open HybridVerify

variable {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω}

/-- Bachelier European call pricing formula. -/
theorem bachelier_call_formula
    {Q : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure Q]
    {S_0 K σ T : ℝ} {Z : Ω → ℝ}
    (h : BachelierHyp Q S_0 K σ T Z) :
    ∫ ω, max (bachelierTerminal S_0 σ T (Z ω) - K) 0 ∂Q
      = (S_0 - K) * Phi (bachelierD S_0 K σ T) +
        σ * Real.sqrt T * gaussianPDFReal 0 1 (bachelierD S_0 K σ T) :=
  HybridVerify.bachelier_call_formula h

metadata.reference: Bachelier (1900) option pricing; modern: Brigo & Mercurio (2006), Black (1976)
metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Parallels bs_call_formula but for arithmetic BM. Key new primitive: truncated mean of N(0,1): integral_id_mul_gaussianPDFReal_Ioi a = pdf(0,1,a), proved via FTC integral_Ioi_of_hasDerivAt_of_tendsto using (-pdf)' = z*pdf. Plus integrable_id_mul_gaussianPDFReal_volume (transfer via withDensity). Lives in lean/HybridVerify/BachelierModel.lean (~330 lines). Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: Implied Volatility Uniqueness
theorems.description: For S, K, T > 0, the BS call price as a function of sigma is strictly monotone on (0, infinity). Hence the implied volatility (when it exists) is unique.

code.lean:
import Mathlib
import HybridVerify.ImpliedVolatility

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real
open HybridVerify

/-- Implied volatility uniqueness: the BS call price is strictly monotone in σ on (0, ∞). -/
theorem implied_vol_unique {K r T : ℝ} (hK : 0 < K) (hT : 0 < T)
    {S : ℝ} (hS : 0 < S) {σ₁ σ₂ : ℝ} (hσ₁ : 0 < σ₁) (hσ₂ : 0 < σ₂)
    (h_eq : HybridVerify.bsV K r σ₁ S T = HybridVerify.bsV K r σ₂ S T) :
    σ₁ = σ₂ :=
  HybridVerify.implied_volatility_unique hK hT hS hσ₁ hσ₂ h_eq

metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Vega-positivity (bsV_vega_pos) + strictMonoOn_of_deriv_pos + StrictMonoOn.injOn. Lives in lean/HybridVerify/ImpliedVolatility.lean. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: Black-76 Formula for Futures Options
theorems.description: European call on a futures contract: V = e^{-rT} [F Phi(d_1) - K Phi(d_2)] where d_1 = (log(F/K) + sigma^2 T/2) / (sigma sqrt(T)) and d_2 = d_1 - sigma sqrt(T). Specialization of BS to zero-drift futures + independent discount rate.

code.lean:
import Mathlib
import HybridVerify.BlackFutures

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real
open scoped NNReal
open HybridVerify

variable {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω}

/-- Black-76 formula for European call on futures. -/
theorem black_futures_call
    {Q : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure Q]
    {F K σ T : ℝ} {Z : Ω → ℝ}
    (h : BSCallHyp Q F K 0 σ T Z) (r : ℝ) :
    ∫ ω, Real.exp (-r * T) * max (bsTerminal F 0 σ T (Z ω) - K) 0 ∂Q
      = Real.exp (-r * T) *
          (F * Phi (bsd1 F K 0 σ T) - K * Phi (bsd2 F K 0 σ T)) :=
  HybridVerify.black_futures_formula h r

metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Specialization of bs_call_formula with BS-drift r=0 (futures are martingales) plus external discount rate. Lives in lean/HybridVerify/BlackFutures.lean. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: Single-Period Binomial Replication Theorem
theorems.description: In a single-period binomial model with no-arbitrage (d < e^r < u), every contingent claim with payoffs (V_u, V_d) in the up/down states is replicable, and the replicating portfolio cost equals the risk-neutral expected payoff discounted: V_0 = e^{-r} (q V_u + (1-q) V_d) where q = (e^r - d) / (u - d).

metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof of all three statements: portfolio cost, up-state payoff, down-state payoff. Lives in lean/HybridVerify/BinomialModel.lean. Axioms-clean. Multi-period backward induction (binomialPrice) also defined and proved consistent; CRR convergence to BS is upstream.

theorems.name: CRR One-Step Risk-Neutral Martingale Identity
theorems.description: Under CRR parameterization (u_n = e^{σ √Δt}, d_n = e^{-σ √Δt}, Δt = T/n), the risk-neutral up-probability p_n = (e^{rΔt} - d_n)/(u_n - d_n) satisfies p_n · u_n + (1 - p_n) · d_n = e^{rΔt}. The discrete-time discounted asset is a Q-martingale at each step. Exact algebraic identity, not asymptotic.

code.lean:
import Mathlib
import HybridVerify.BinomialCRRConvergence

open HybridVerify

/-- CRR one-step risk-neutral martingale identity. -/
theorem crr_one_step_martingale_identity {σ T r : ℝ} {n : ℕ}
    (h_du : crrDown σ T n < crrUp σ T n) :
    crrProb r σ T n * crrUp σ T n + (1 - crrProb r σ T n) * crrDown σ T n
      = Real.exp (crrPerStepRate r T n) :=
  HybridVerify.crr_one_step_martingale h_du

metadata.reference: CRR parameterization, one-step martingale (Hull 21.1, Shreve I 1.2)
metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Pure algebraic identity following from the definitions of crrUp, crrDown, crrPerStepRate, crrProb. Lives in lean/HybridVerify/BinomialCRRConvergence.lean. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: CRR Risk-Neutral Probability Tends to 1/2
theorems.description: Under CRR parameterization, p_n → 1/2 as n → ∞. This is the substantive analytic step in the CRR-to-BS correspondence: the per-step Bernoulli increment becomes asymptotically symmetric. Implies the variance limit n · σ² Δt · 4 p_n (1 - p_n) → σ² T.

code.lean:
import Mathlib
import HybridVerify.BinomialCRRConvergence

open HybridVerify Filter
open scoped Topology

/-- CRR risk-neutral probability tends to 1/2 as n → ∞. -/
theorem crr_prob_tendsto_half {σ T r : ℝ} (hσ : 0 < σ) (hT : 0 < T) :
    Filter.Tendsto (fun n : ℕ => crrProb r σ T n) Filter.atTop (𝓝 (1/2)) :=
  HybridVerify.crrProb_tendsto_half hσ hT

metadata.reference: CRR convergence (analytic step), Hull 21.1, Shreve I 3.2
metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Substantial: ~80 lines using difference-quotient limits (e^{cx}-1)/x → c via hasDerivAt_iff_tendsto_slope, plus quotient-of-limits arithmetic to show p(h) → 1/2 as h → 0, plus composition with h_n = √(T/n). Implies the variance limit. Lives in lean/HybridVerify/BinomialCRRConvergence.lean. Axioms-clean. (Full distributional convergence binomialPrice → bs_call_price is upstream-gated on triangular-array CLT.)

theorems.name: CRR Variance Limit
theorems.description: Under CRR parameterization, n · σ² · (T/n) · 4 p_n (1 - p_n) → σ² T as n → ∞. The per-step variance of the log-return matches the BS variance to leading order. Direct corollary of crrProb_tendsto_half.

code.lean:
import Mathlib
import HybridVerify.BinomialCRRConvergence

open HybridVerify Filter
open scoped Topology

/-- CRR variance limit: 4 σ² T · p_n (1 - p_n) → σ² T. -/
theorem crr_variance_limit_theorem {σ T r : ℝ} (hσ : 0 < σ) (hT : 0 < T) :
    Filter.Tendsto
      (fun n : ℕ => 4 * σ^2 * T * (crrProb r σ T n) * (1 - crrProb r σ T n))
      Filter.atTop (𝓝 (σ^2 * T)) :=
  HybridVerify.crr_variance_limit hσ hT

metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Direct corollary of crrProb_tendsto_half via 4 p_n (1 - p_n) → 4 · (1/2) · (1/2) = 1, then const_mul to get σ² T. Lives in lean/HybridVerify/BinomialCRRConvergence.lean. Axioms-clean.
